Fresh Install

Set up MoltenVR from scratch

A clean Mac to VR streaming in about five minutes. The setup wizard does the heavy lifting.
1

Download & open MoltenVR

Grab MoltenVR-Preview-vX.Y.Z.zip from the GitHub Releases page, unzip, drag MoltenVR.app to /Applications, and open it (right-click → Open the first time — the preview isn't notarized yet). The setup wizard appears on first launch.

2

System check

The wizard verifies the streaming bridge and OpenXR runtime (bundled — always green on a release build) and looks for optional extras: adb for USB streaming, BlackHole for game audio. Anything missing shows the exact brew command that fixes it. Audio and USB are optional — Wi-Fi streaming works without them. Hit Continue.

The system check — red rows show the copy-paste fix, and nothing optional blocks you.
Setting up the optional extras — all three come from Homebrew; if brew isn't installed yet, run the one-liner from brew.sh first. Then, in Terminal:

USB streaming (adb): brew install android-platform-tools — then plug the headset in, put it on, and tap Allow USB debugging (tick "Always allow") when the headset asks.

Game audio (BlackHole): brew install --cask blackhole-2ch — macOS will ask you to approve the audio driver in System Settings, and it needs a reboot to show up.

Audio routing (SwitchAudioSource): brew install switchaudio-osx — no reboot needed.

Relaunch MoltenVR (or hit Back and forward again) and the checks re-run green.
MoltenVR — Fresh Install Setup
Page 1 of 3
3

Download Wine — one time, one click

Games need a Windows environment (a bottle). On a fresh Mac the wizard offers Download Wine (one-time) — about 330 MB, managed entirely by MoltenVR. No Whisky, no CrossOver, nothing else to install.

First run: no bottles yet — the wizard offers the download right there.
4

Create your bottle

When the download finishes the field flips to Create Bottle. Keep the default name or pick your own, click it, and give the Windows prefix a minute to initialise.

Wine installed — name the bottle and click Create Bottle.
MoltenVR — Fresh Install Setup
Page 2 of 3
5

Install the VR runtime

The new bottle appears with DXMT ✓ already lit — MoltenVR's Wine ships the graphics layer built in. Click Install Runtime and the row shows DXMT ✓ Runtime ✓: the bottle is VR-ready. Hit Continue, then Start using MoltenVR.

One click later the bottle is VR-ready — DXMT and the OpenXR runtime both checked.
6

Stream

On the Play screen press Start Stream, put the headset on, and open the ALVR app (v20.14.0). Pick USB (smoother, needs adb) or Wi-Fi. From here: install Steam into the bottle from the Bottles tab and launch games from the Games tab.

Streaming live — game audio, bitrate and resolution are all adjustable mid-stream.
Next: the Beat Saber Setup Guide walks through installing Steam and your first game, and Bottles & DXMT explains what's under the hood. Both are in docs/guides and on Discord.
